title: Enums 2
description: Enum variants can hold data of different types. Each variant can have its own structure.
instructions: Define enum variants that hold different types of data.
hints: Each variant can hold different data: struct-like fields, tuple-like fields, or no data.
Move uses named fields: Move { x: i32, y: i32 }
Echo uses a single String: Echo(String), ChangeColor uses a tuple: ChangeColor(i32, i32, i32)
explanation: Rust enums are very powerful - each variant can hold different types and amounts of data. A variant can have named fields (like a struct), unnamed fields (like a tuple), or no fields at all. This makes Rust enums similar to 'tagged unions' or 'algebraic data types' in other languages. You can also implement methods on enums just like structs.
